segments.text: Lesson 48.

translations.zh-Hans: 第48课。

segments.text: Planning a Share Portfolio.

translations.zh-Hans: 规划股票投资组合。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: How does the older investor differ in his approach to investment from the younger investor?

translations.zh-Hans: 年长投资者与年轻投资者的投资方式有何不同？

segments.text: There is no shortage of tipsters around offering get-rich-quick opportunities.

translations.zh-Hans: 周围从不缺少提供快速致富机会的荐股者。

segments.text: But if you are a private investor, leave the Las Vegas mentality to those with money to fritter.

translations.zh-Hans: 但如果你是私人投资者，就把拉斯维加斯式心态留给那些有钱可挥霍的人。

segments.text: The serious investor needs a proper portfolio, a well-planned selection of investments, with a definite structure and a clear aim.

translations.zh-Hans: 严肃投资者需要一个适当的投资组合，即结构明确、目标清晰、经过周密规划的投资选择。

segments.text: But exactly how does a newcomer to the stock market go about achieving that?

translations.zh-Hans: 可是股市新手究竟该怎样做到这一点？

segments.text: Well, if you go to five reputable stockbrokers and ask them what you should do with your money, you're likely to get five different answers, even if you give all the relevant information about your age, family, finances, and what you want

translations.zh-Hans: 如果你去找五位信誉良好的股票经纪人，问该如何处理资金，你很可能得到五个不同答案，即使你把年龄、家庭、财务状况以及希望

segments.text: from your investments.

translations.zh-Hans: 从投资中获得什么等所有相关信息都告诉他们。

segments.text: Moral?

translations.zh-Hans: 教训是什么？

segments.text: There is no one right way to structure a portfolio.

translations.zh-Hans: 构建投资组合没有唯一正确的方法。

segments.text: However, there are undoubtedly some wrong ways, and you can be sure that none of our five advisors would have suggested sinking all, or perhaps any of your money, into parawigs.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，肯定存在一些错误方法；可以确信，五位顾问都不会建议你把全部、甚至任何资金投进“佩里维格斯”公司。

segments.text: So, what should you do?

translations.zh-Hans: 那么你该怎么办？

segments.text: We'll assume that you have sorted out the basics, like mortgages, pensions, and access to sufficient cash reserves.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们假设你已处理好抵押贷款、养老金和充足现金储备等基本问题。

segments.text: You should then establish your own individual aims.

translations.zh-Hans: 接下来应确立自己的个人目标。

segments.text: These are partly a matter of personal circumstances, partly a matter of psychology.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些目标一部分取决于个人处境，一部分取决于心理。

segments.text: For instance, if you are older, you have less time to recover from any major losses, and you may well wish to boost your pension income.

translations.zh-Hans: 例如，年纪较大时，你从重大损失中恢复的时间较少，也很可能希望增加养老金收入。

segments.text: So, preserving your capital and generating extra income are your main priorities.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，保全资本和获得额外收入是首要任务。

segments.text: In this case, you'd probably construct a portfolio with some shares, but not high-risk ones, along with gilts, cash deposits, and perhaps convertibles or the income shares of split capital investment trusts.

translations.zh-Hans: 这种情况下，你的投资组合可能包括一些非高风险股票，以及金边债券、现金存款，也许还有可转换证券或分割资本投资信托的收益股。

segments.text: If you are younger and in a solid financial position, you may decide to take an aggressive approach, but only if you're blessed with a sanguine disposition and won't suffer sleepless nights over share prices.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果你较年轻、财务状况稳固，可以决定采取进取策略，但前提是天性乐观，不会因股价而彻夜难眠。

segments.text: If you recognize yourself in this description, you might include a couple of heady growth stocks in your portfolio, alongside your more pedestrian investments.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果这描述符合你，可以在较稳健的投资旁加入两三只令人兴奋的成长股。

segments.text: Once you have decided on your investment aims, you can then decide where to put your money.

translations.zh-Hans: 确定投资目标后，就可以决定把钱投向哪里。

segments.text: The golden rule here is spread your risk.

translations.zh-Hans: 这里的黄金法则是分散风险。

segments.text: If you put all of your money into Periwigs International, you're setting yourself up as a hostage to fortune.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果把所有钱都投进“佩里维格斯国际”，你就把自己变成了任凭命运摆布的人质。
